title: Tall Nettles by Edward Thomas — Nature Poetry Lines
content:
TALL nettles cover up, as they have done
These many springs, the rusty harrow, the plough
Long worn out, and the roller made of stone:
Only the elm butt tops the nettles now.

This corner of the farmyard I like most:
As well as any bloom upon a flower
I like the dust on the nettles, never lost
Except to prove the sweetness of a shower.
meaning: "Tall Nettles" by Edward Thomas is a nature poem consisting of 9 lines. The poet explores themes of nature through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "TALL nettles cover up, as they have done These many springs, the rusty harrow, the plough...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Edward Thomas's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
